what_it_does: Renders a single HTML composition titled "One Motif, Three Tellings" with three side-by-side stages showing a motif moving from a scattered mesh, to an inward hub, to an outward hub with role labels, using inline CSS and generated SVG/diagram markup from DNA.org helpers.
observations: Defines three absolutely positioned stage panels with radial gradient backgrounds and shared caption, diagram, and description structure.
Stage 1 injects DNA.org.mesh with a fixed seed and density.
Stage 2 injects DNA.org.hubNetwork with an octagon hub and inward direction.
Stage 3 injects DNA.org.hubNetwork with an octagon hub, outward direction, and node labels D, A, C, M, S, F.
Adds two circular SVG arrow controls between panels by assigning the same SVG string to ar1 and ar2.
Inline comments explicitly describe the intended motif progression and transport arrows.
standouts: The page is structured as a three-step visual progression: scattered, drawn to a centre, returned in order.
The same hub motif is reused with changed directionality between the second and third panels.
The visual language reserves the gold accent for arrows and the resolved core language.
conventions: Single-file HTML piece with inline CSS and inline script.
Uses CSS custom properties supplied by the shared surface stylesheet.
Uses class names for layout variants and IDs for generated diagram targets.
Uses innerHTML injection for generated organism markup and arrow SVG.
Follows a narrative composition pattern with title, lede, staged panels, foot, and mark elements.
concerns: The layout relies on absolute positioning and fixed panel widths, which may be sensitive to narrow or unusual viewport sizes.
The file depends on /surface/styles/piece.css and /surface/runtime/organisms.js being available at runtime.
The generated diagrams and arrows appear visually driven, with no explicit accessibility labels or fallback text.
innerHTML assignment assumes DNA.org helper output and the inline SVG string are valid and safe.
notes: A staged motif explainer page that uses generated diagrams to show scattering, gathering, and ordered return.
questions: What do the third-stage role labels D, A, C, M, S, and F stand for from this file alone?
What viewport size is this composition designed for?
Should the empty hub labels and empty stage-two node labels remain intentionally blank?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in comments and visible copy matches the implementation: the file depicts one motif recurring and transforming across three stages.
apparent_themes: visual narrative through repeated diagram forms
intention_signals: It seems intended to communicate a design idea where disconnected work becomes gathered into a shared core and then distributed back into role-specific views.
novelty: The file combines narrative labels, generated organism diagrams, and transition arrows into a compact visual argument about one motif changing state.
contribution: Contributes a three-stage motif ledger entry: mesh as dispersed activity, inward octagon hub as gathered core, outward octagon hub as role-directed return, plus the visual transition language linking those states.
summary_for_embedding: A single HTML/CSS/JS visual piece titled "One Motif, Three Tellings" that renders three generated diagram panels showing a motif moving from scattered mesh to inward hub to outward role-labeled hub.
intention_for_embedding: This was for expressing how one recurring design shape transforms across a telling: scattered work gathers to a core and returns outward in ordered roles.
